seo.title: Streaming LLM Responses implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping Streaming LLM Responses projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist outlines the technical requirements for deploying robust streaming LLM interfaces, covering infrastructure configuration, client-side rendering performance, and error resilience.

items.title: Disable Proxy Buffering
items.description: Ensure Nginx or other reverse proxies have 'proxy_buffering off' set to prevent the server from waiting for the full response before sending chunks to the client.

items.title: Set Event-Stream Headers
items.description: Verify the server response includes 'Content-Type: text/event-stream' and 'Cache-Control: no-cache, no-transform' to maintain an open persistent connection.

items.title: Configure Edge Function Timeouts
items.description: Adjust the maximum execution time for Vercel or Netlify functions to exceed the expected maximum LLM generation time (typically 30-60 seconds).

items.title: Enforce HTTP/2 or HTTP/3
items.description: Confirm the environment supports HTTP/2 to bypass the 6-connection limit per domain inherent in HTTP/1.1, which causes stream blocking.

items.title: Bypass Compression for SSE
items.description: Check that Gzip or Brotli compression is disabled for the event-stream path, as compression algorithms often buffer data until a specific threshold is met.

items.title: Implement Partial JSON Parsing
items.description: Use a specialized parser like 'best-effort-json-parser' to render UI components from structured data before the closing brace arrives.

items.title: Handle Multi-byte UTF-8 Chunks
items.description: Verify the decoder handles characters split across two chunks using TextDecoder to prevent rendering garbled text symbols.

items.title: Validate Stream Termination
items.description: Detect the '[DONE]' signal or null chunk explicitly to trigger final state updates and close the ReadableStream object.

items.title: Sanitize Streamed HTML
items.description: Apply a streaming-safe HTML sanitizer if rendering markdown with embedded HTML to prevent XSS during the incremental render phase.

items.title: Buffer Small Chunks
items.description: Implement a minor client-side buffer (e.g., 10-20ms) to group extremely rapid individual character updates into larger UI paint cycles.

items.title: Implement Auto-Scroll Override
items.description: Disable automatic scrolling to the bottom if the user manually scrolls up to read previous content during a stream.

items.title: Add Visual Cursor Indicator
items.description: Render a blinking cursor or distinct 'typing' animation at the end of the current text chunk to indicate the process is active.

items.title: Optimistic UI for Prompts
items.description: Immediately append the user message to the chat history before the stream request initiates to provide instant feedback.

items.title: ARIA Live Region Support
items.description: Configure ARIA live regions or specialized screen reader announcements to notify assistive technology users of incoming text without focus shifts.

items.title: Markdown Incremental Rendering
items.description: Use a markdown library that supports partial tokens to prevent code blocks or tables from breaking visually while they are being streamed.

items.title: Client-Side Reconnection Logic
items.description: Implement exponential backoff for stream interruptions to resume the connection without losing the existing UI state.

items.title: Handle Mid-Stream Rate Limits
items.description: Catch 429 errors occurring mid-stream and display a specific 'Rate limit reached' message rather than a generic network error.

items.title: Explicit Abort Controller
items.description: Provide a 'Stop Generating' button that calls 'abort()' on the fetch request to immediately kill the stream and free up browser resources.

items.title: Cleanup on Unmount
items.description: Ensure the AbortController is triggered in the useEffect cleanup phase to prevent memory leaks and background network activity.

items.title: Fallback to Non-Streaming
items.description: Implement a fallback mechanism that switches to standard JSON fetch if the browser or network environment does not support ReadableStream.

items.title: Track Time to First Token (TTFT)
items.description: Log the duration between the request initiation and the arrival of the first data chunk to monitor model and cold-start latency.

items.title: Monitor Tokens Per Second (TPS)
items.description: Calculate and record the average throughput of the stream to identify performance degradation in specific geographic regions.

items.title: Trace ID Correlation
items.description: Pass a unique request ID through the SSE headers to correlate client-side stream errors with backend LLM provider logs.

items.title: Avoid State Re-renders
items.description: Use refs or optimized state management (e.g., useReducer) to prevent the entire component tree from re-rendering on every token arrival.

items.title: Audit Stream Bundle Impact
items.description: Verify that streaming libraries like 'ai' or 'eventsource-parser' do not exceed the project's third-party JS budget.
